title: Iran says train service resumed on routes damaged by strikes during war
description:
Authorities say tracks rebuilt in key areas; IDF had said it hit specific rail sections as part of effort to prevent Revolutionary Guards from moving weapon systems
The post Iran says train service resumed on routes damaged by strikes during war appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Daily Briefing Apr. 13 – Trump plays chicken with Iran over Strait of Hormuz
description:
Diplomatic correspondent Lazar Berman on the failed US-Iran talks in Islamabad, tomorrow's planned Israel-Lebanon talks in DC and ripple effects from the defeat of Hungary's longtime leader
The post Daily Briefing Apr. 13  Trump plays chicken with Iran over Strait of Hormuz appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: New Details Emerge About Rescue Mission Deep Inside Iran
description: New details were released during a White House press conference which described the rescue of the crew of DUDE 44, the F-15E Strike Eagle that went down in Iran. The rescue of the crew of the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet shot down over Iran has rapidly emerged as one of the most complex and []

title: What is Sweden’s Gute II concept?
description: Sweden's Gute II concept builds on a layered C-UAS system proven in Gotland in June last year, where conscripts tested several systems.

title: Iran’s other would-be WMD program lies in ruins following strikes by Israel and the US
description: “It almost seems like an afterthought and lower priority to the war planners,” said Jim Lamson, a former Iran analyst for the CIA of 23 years.

title: ‘A whole civilization will die tonight,’ Trump says as Iran defies deal
description: A former U.S. State Department legal advisor said the remarks “could plausibly be interpreted as a threat to commit genocide.”

title: Trump weighs pulling some US troops from Europe amid NATO strains, official says
description: U.S. President Donald Trump has discussed with advisers the option of removing some U.S. troops from Europe, a senior White House official told Reuters.

title: REPLAY: Pope Leo XIV delivers remarks as he opens trip in Algeria
description: Pope Leo XIV on Monday began a landmark visit to Algeria, where he called for forgiveness in a speech paying tribute to victims of the country's war of independence from France (1954-1962). The pontiff called for peace in all nations to prevail, in a trip overshadowed by the US-Israeli war in Iran and an extraordinary broadside against Leo by President Donald Trump.

title: Pope Leo says has 'no intention to debate' with Trump
description: Pope Leo XIV on Monday said he had "no intention to debate" with Donald Trump, after the US president criticised him over his comments on the US-Iran war. "I am not a politician," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane as they headed to Algeria for the pontiff's first visit to Africa. "I have no intention to debate with (Trump). The message is the same: to promote peace."

title: The digital euro: Europe’s bid for financial sovereignty
description: Behind most card payments in Europe stand two American giants, Visa and Mastercard – a dependence recently thrust into the spotlight. After issuing an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, several judges at the International Criminal Court were sanctioned by Washington, leaving them unable to use their bank cards – even on European soil.

title: French cement-maker Lafarge found guilty of financing jihadists in Syria
description: ​A Paris court ​on Monday found cement maker Lafarge, now owned by Switzerland's Holcim, guilty ​of ‌paying the Islamic State (IS) group and other jihadists to ⁠keep a plant operating in northern Syria ​during the country's ‌civil war.

title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
description: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

title: Tear gas fired at India workers demanding higher wages as living costs rise
description: Factory workers protest in national capitals suburb of Noida as living costs across the world rise due to the Iran war.

title: How the US-Iran talks in Islamabad unfolded
description: Pakistan has been under the spotlight as it managed what others couldn’t due to its ties with the Gulf, US and China.

title: Iran’s army says US plan to blockade Hormuz ‘amounts to piracy’
description: The US restrictions on maritime navigation and transit in international waters are illegal, Iranian military says.

title: Israeli forces fire tear gas near Palestinian schoolchildren
description: Israeli forces fired tear gas towards a group of children trying to walk to school in the occupied West Bank.

title: Starmer says UK will not support US blockade of Strait of Hormuz
description: Other US allies criticise Trumps move, including France, Spain and Turkiye, and China also condemns the plan.

title: How a UK military college sought to distance itself from ban on Israelis
description: Head of defence college asked military official to ensure government took ownership of ban, emails reveal.

title: Hungary May No Longer Be Putin’s Ally, but It Can’t Afford a Full Break
description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat will make it harder for the Kremlin to divide Europe, but Moscow retains leverage through energy sales.

title: The ‘Islamabad Peace Talks’ Are Over. What Now for Pakistan?
description: With the U.S. and Iranian delegations gone from Islamabad and the cease-fire uncertain, Pakistan faces its own economic and diplomatic challenges.
